title: Logic
description: Schema for a logic specification object. Logic objects represent formal or informal specifications used for preconditions (requires), postconditions (guarantees), safety rules, invariants, guards, and derivation policies. A logic object must have a description and may optionally include a formal notation and specification. If validation fails, verify 'description' is present and non-empty.

type.description: The kind of logic specification. Optional; when omitted the converter assigns the correct kind based on context. Valid values: 'assessment' (boolean check, no primed values), 'state_change' (attribute assignment — the target field holds the attribute being set, the specification holds only the right-hand-side value expression with no primed values), 'query' (named return value — the target field holds the output identifier, the specification holds the value expression with no primed values), 'safety_rule' (boolean constraint that MAY reference primed values like inventory' >= 0 to express constraints over both prior and new state), 'value' (single unnamed value expression), 'let' (local variable definition: target = expression).

description.description: A human-readable description of the logic specification (e.g., 'Order total must be positive', 'User must be authenticated'). This is required and must be non-empty. If validation fails: ensure this is a string with at least one character.

target.description: The target identifier for this logic. The target separates the left-hand side from the specification so that the specification contains only the right-hand-side value expression (no primed values). Required for 'state_change' type (the lowercase attribute SubKey being set, e.g., 'shipping', 'status' — so 'self.log' = Append(self.log, amount)' becomes target='log', specification='Append(self.log, amount)'). Required for 'query' type (the output identifier name, e.g., 'result'; can be mixed case but cannot start with '_'). Required for 'let' type (the local variable name, cannot start with '_'). Must be empty or omitted for 'assessment', 'safety_rule', and 'value' types.

target_type_spec.description: Optional type specification for the target. Declares the TLA+ type of the logic's result (e.g., 'Int', 'STRING', 'SUBSET STRING'). Only meaningful for logic types that have a target ('state_change', 'query', 'let').

notation.description: Optional formal notation system used for the specification. Currently the only supported value is 'tla_plus' (TLA+ notation). When present, the specification field should contain a formal expression in this notation. If validation fails: ensure this is exactly 'tla_plus'.

specification.description: Optional formal specification in the given notation. This is a machine-readable expression that formally defines the logic. For 'state_change' and 'query' types this must be only the right-hand-side value expression (no primed values) because the left-hand side is captured by the 'target' field. For 'safety_rule' type, primed values ARE allowed (e.g., 'inventory' >= 0') to express constraints over both prior and new state. For 'assessment' and 'value' types, no primed values. Should only be present when notation is also specified.
